description_long: This is a double-ended open-ended wrench, commonly used for tightening or loosening nuts and bolts. The tool is made of solid metal, likely steel, and features two U-shaped jaws of different sizes at each end, allowing for versatility in fitting various fastener sizes. The body is straight and flat, with a slight taper toward the center, providing strength and leverage while remaining lightweight. The surface appears matte gray, with no visible branding or decorative elements, and the handle is smooth without any additional grip pattern. Both jaws are fixed and parallel, designed for a secure grip on hexagonal fasteners. The overall appearance is utilitarian and robust, suitable for use in garages, workshops, or urban maintenance settings.
description: A solid, gray, double-ended open-ended wrench with two different-sized fixed jaws and a smooth, flat handle. The tool is robust and utilitarian, made entirely of metal.
description_view_0: Angled view showing both open jaws and the flat, tapered handle.
description_view_1: Side view displaying the thin profile and flatness of the wrench.
description_view_2: Opposite angled view highlighting the open jaws and the straight handle.
description_view_3: Edge-on view emphasizing the narrow width and uniform thickness of the wrench.
